prevalence.value: common; ~50-70% of children have at least one episode by age 3
prevalence.trend: stable; pneumococcal vaccination reduces some bacterial burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Otoscopy evidence of effusion and inflammation; symptom onset and severity guide management; stewardship reduces unnecessary antibiotics

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with guideline-based analgesia and selective antibiotics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to otoscopy and follow-up; ENT referral pathways common for recurrent cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Analgesia; selective antibiotics; tympanostomy pathways for recurrent disease when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Maintaining stewardship and managing recurrent disease burden
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong primary care and ENT access in many systems
